title: Measles Outbreak in England Leads to Two Child Deaths
subtitle: Health officials urge parents to vaccinate children as cases continue to rise across the country.

summary.en: Two children in England have died from measles this year, marking a tragic turn in the current health crisis. Health authorities report over 100 new infections in the last two weeks alone. Most of those affected are unvaccinated children aged 10 and under. Experts warn that the disease is spreading easily and can lead to serious complications. Officials are now calling for parents to ensure their children receive the necessary MMR vaccines.
summary.zh: 今年英格蘭有兩名兒童死於麻疹，這標誌著當前健康危機的悲劇性轉折。衛生當局報告僅在過去兩週內就新增了超過一百起感染病例。受影響者大多是十歲以下未接種疫苗的兒童。專家警告，該疾病極易傳播且可能導致嚴重併發症。官員們目前正呼籲家長確保子女接種必要的MMR疫苗。

paragraphs.en: Health officials in England have confirmed that two children have died from measles this year. This tragic news comes as new data shows more than 100 cases reported in the last two weeks. According to The Guardian Health, this is the first time in eight years that two children have died from the disease in a single year in England and Wales. One child died from acute measles, while the other passed away due to the late effects of the virus.
paragraphs.zh: 英國衛生官員證實，今年已有兩名兒童死於麻疹。這項悲劇性的消息傳出之際，最新數據顯示過去兩週內通報了超過一百起病例。根據《衛報健康版》（The Guardian Health）報導，這是英格蘭與威爾斯地區八年來首次在單一年份內有兩名兒童因該疾病死亡。其中一名兒童死於急性麻疹，另一名則是因為病毒的後遺症而不幸離世。

paragraphs.en: The UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) reports that the virus continues to move through many parts of the country. Recent data shows that cases are highest in London, the east of England, and the West Midlands. Between January 1 and June 8, there were 736 confirmed cases. This is a significant concern, as the total number of cases for the entire year of 2025 was 959. Most of the people affected are children aged 10 and under who have not received the required vaccines.
paragraphs.zh: 英國衛生安全局（UKHSA）指出，病毒持續在該國多個地區傳播。近期數據顯示，倫敦、英格蘭東部以及西密德蘭地區的病例數最高。從一月一日到六月八日，累計確診病例達七百三十六起。這令人相當擔憂，因為二零二五年全年的病例總數為九百五十九起。受影響者多為十歲以下且未接種必要疫苗的兒童。

paragraphs.en: Measles is a highly infectious viral illness. While many people recover, the disease can cause serious health problems. These include pneumonia, which is a lung infection, and brain inflammation. In rare cases, it can lead to long-term disability or even death. Dr. Vanessa Saliba, a consultant at the UKHSA, expressed deep sadness for the families who lost their children. She noted that measles is not a harmless childhood illness and can be very serious.
paragraphs.zh: 麻疹是一種傳染性極強的病毒性疾病。雖然許多人能夠康復，但該疾病可能引發嚴重的健康問題，包括肺炎（一種肺部感染）以及腦炎。在極少數情況下，它可能導致長期殘疾甚至死亡。英國衛生安全局的顧問凡妮莎·薩利巴（Vanessa Saliba）醫師對失去孩子的家庭表達了深切的哀悼。她強調，麻疹並非無害的兒童疾病，其後果可能非常嚴重。

paragraphs.en: To prevent the spread of the virus, health experts are urging all parents to make sure their children are up to date with their MMR or MMRV vaccines. These vaccines provide the safest and best protection against measles. The MMR vaccine protects against measles, mumps, and rubella. For those who have missed their shots, it is possible to catch up at any age by visiting a local doctor. Getting vaccinated does more than just protect the individual; it also helps keep babies who are too young for the vaccine safe, along with people who cannot have the vaccine due to other health conditions.
paragraphs.zh: 為了防止病毒擴散，衛生專家呼籲所有家長務必確保子女已接種麻疹、腮腺炎、德國麻疹（MMR）或麻疹、腮腺炎、德國麻疹、水痘（MMRV）疫苗。這些疫苗提供了對抗麻疹最安全且最有效的保護。MMR疫苗能預防麻疹、腮腺炎與德國麻疹。對於錯過接種時間的人，任何年齡層都可以透過諮詢當地醫師來補打。接種疫苗不僅能保護個人，還能保護年紀太小而無法接種的嬰兒，以及因其他健康狀況而無法接種疫苗的人群。

paragraphs.en: There is growing concern about the current vaccination rates in England. Earlier this year, experts told The Guardian Health that the National Health Service (NHS) was failing to ensure children received their vaccines. Some areas of England now have vaccination rates similar to those found in Afghanistan and Malawi. Recent data shows that only 91.8% of five-year-olds had received one dose of the MMR vaccine in 2024-25. This is the lowest level since 2010-11 and shows that vaccination coverage has stalled.
paragraphs.zh: 英格蘭目前的疫苗接種率令人日益擔憂。今年稍早，專家曾向《衛報健康版》表示，國家醫療保健服務（NHS）未能確保兒童按時接種疫苗。英格蘭部分地區目前的疫苗接種率，甚至與阿富汗和馬拉威等國相當。近期數據顯示，二零二四至二零二五年度，僅有百分之九十一點八的五歲兒童接種了一劑MMR疫苗。這是自二零一零至二零一一年度以來的最低水準，顯示疫苗覆蓋率已陷入停滯。

paragraphs.en: James Murray, the health secretary, spoke about the situation, noting that these deaths are a heartbreaking reminder of the dangers of the disease. He emphasized that the MMR vaccine has saved many lives and remains the most effective tool against this outbreak. He urged parents and caregivers to check their children’s vaccination status immediately. He stated that it is never too late to catch up on missed doses to protect both their families and the most vulnerable members of the community.
paragraphs.zh: 衛生大臣詹姆斯·莫瑞（James Murray）針對此情況表示，這些死亡案例令人心碎，也提醒了人們這種疾病的危險性。他強調，MMR疫苗拯救了許多生命，至今仍是對抗此次疫情最有效的工具。他敦促家長與照護者立即檢查子女的疫苗接種紀錄。他表示，補打疫苗永遠不嫌晚，這不僅能保護家人，也能保護社區中最脆弱的成員。

paragraphs.en: Public health experts continue to monitor the situation closely. The goal is to increase vaccination rates to stop the virus from spreading further. As the UKHSA continues to track new infections, the message from officials remains clear: do not delay, and book an appointment for vaccination today. Protecting the population against this preventable disease is a top priority for health authorities as they work to manage this ongoing public health challenge.
paragraphs.zh: 公共衛生專家持續密切監控疫情。目標是提高疫苗接種率，以阻止病毒進一步擴散。隨著英國衛生安全局持續追蹤新感染病例，官員們的訊息非常明確：切勿拖延，請立即預約接種疫苗。在處理這項持續性的公共衛生挑戰時，保護民眾免受這種可預防疾病的侵害，是衛生當局的首要任務。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To stop making progress or to stop moving forward.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於指車輛熄火，這裡指疫苗接種率停止成長。文中：This is the lowest level since 2010-11 and shows that vaccination coverage has stalled.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To do something that you did not have time to do earlier.
keyTerms.noteZh: 不僅指追上某人，這裡指補打疫苗。文中：For those who have missed their shots, it is possible to catch up at any age by visiting a local doctor.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To spread or travel from one place to another.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見指身體移動，這裡指病毒在人群中傳播。文中：The UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) reports that the virus continues to move through many parts of the country.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Two children die from measles as England data shows 100 new infections

previousArticles.title: Measles Outbreak in England Leads to Two Child Deaths
previousArticles.summaryEn: Two children in England have died from measles this year, marking a tragic development in the ongoing outbreak. Health authorities report over 100 new cases in the last two weeks alone. Most of those affected are children aged 10 and under who have not received the necessary vaccines. Experts are calling for urgent action to improve vaccination coverage. The situation highlights the serious risks of a disease that was once considered under control.

previousArticles.title: Measles Outbreak Nears Worst Levels in Decades
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United States is currently facing a major measles outbreak with over 2,000 confirmed cases this year. Experts warn that the actual number of infections is likely much higher due to limited tracking. The virus is spreading quickly in communities where vaccination rates have fallen over the last two decades. Public health departments are struggling to manage the crisis because of limited funding and political challenges. Doctors are now calling for better leadership and clearer communication to help protect vulnerable children.

quiz.question: How did the two children who died from measles this year pass away?

options.A: Both children died from acute measles infections.
options.B: One died from acute measles, and the other from the late effects of the virus.
options.C: Both children died due to complications from pneumonia caused by the virus.
options.D: One died from brain inflammation, and the other from a lack of vaccination.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段最後一句話：「One child died from acute measles, while the other passed away due to the late effects of the virus.」明確指出兩位兒童的死因不同。選項 A 錯誤，因為只有一位是急性感染；選項 C 雖提到肺炎是麻疹併發症，但並非文中描述兩位兒童的具體死因；選項 D 的腦部發炎雖是併發症，但文中並未將其歸類為這兩位兒童的特定死因。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the current state of vaccination in England based on the text?

options.A: The government has successfully reached its target vaccination rate for 2025.
options.B: Vaccination rates are currently higher in England than in developing nations.
options.C: The decline in vaccination rates has made the community more vulnerable to outbreaks.
options.D: Most parents in England are actively refusing to vaccinate their children due to personal beliefs.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第五段提到疫苗接種率停滯，且與阿富汗和馬拉威等國相當，結合第四段提到疫苗能保護無法接種的脆弱群體，可推論出接種率下降導致群體免疫力減弱，使社區更易受病毒威脅。選項 A 與文中提到的「failing to ensure」矛盾；選項 B 錯誤，文中提到部分地區接種率已降至與開發中國家相當；選項 D 文中並未提及家長拒絕的原因，僅提到接種率下降的現象。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'stalled' mean as it is used in the context of vaccination coverage?

options.A: It has suddenly increased at a rapid pace.
options.B: It has stopped making progress or improving.
options.C: It has been officially cancelled by the government.
options.D: It has become very difficult to measure accurately.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在文中「vaccination coverage has stalled」是指疫苗接種覆蓋率停滯不前，沒有進展。stalled 在此處意指進度受阻或停頓。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 C 過度詮釋，文中並未說政策被取消；選項 D 雖然數據可能有挑戰，但這不是 stalled 在此處的定義，此處強調的是進度上的停滯。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: To provide a medical history of measles and its symptoms over the last decade.
options.B: To criticize the National Health Service for its handling of the 2025 outbreak.
options.C: To explain the scientific differences between the MMR and MMRV vaccines.
options.D: To highlight the dangers of rising measles cases and encourage urgent vaccination.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。本文主要報導了麻疹病例增加及死亡案例，強調其危險性，並多次引用官員呼籲家長儘速為兒童補打疫苗，這是文章的核心目的。選項 A 僅是背景資訊；選項 B 雖有提到 NHS 的不足，但並非全文主旨；選項 C 文中僅簡單提及疫苗種類，並非討論重點。
